fault_hypothesis: Line 66 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`> limit`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>= limit`), so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns True instead of False.

constraints.intent: When recent count equals the limit, the condition must be triggered (>= not >).
After the if-branch is skipped, recent count must be strictly less than limit (not equal), otherwise the comparison operator is wrong.
Before using recent in the comparison, if count meets or exceeds limit the strict > must also hold — this will be violated when count == limit.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result is True) or len(recent__1) < limit

constraints.intent: The function should only return True when recent count is strictly below the limit.
